title: State of Decay 3" Tops "Halo" on Steam Wishlists
body:
In a shocking turn of events, the upcoming video game State of Decay 3 has garnered more wishlists on Steam than two of Xbox's most iconic franchises, Halo and Gears of War. This unexpected development has left many in the gaming industry wondering how Microsoft, the parent company of Xbox, allowed such a valuable property to slip through its fingers. The fact that State of Decay 3 was on the verge of cancellation before being rescued by a new buyer is a stark reminder of Microsoft's sometimes shortsighted approach to its gaming division.

The recent "big reset" at Xbox, which involved a significant reduction in force across the company, has sent shockwaves throughout the gaming community. The cuts, led by Microsoft's CFO, were aimed at streamlining the company's operations and refocusing its efforts on more lucrative ventures. However, the decision to put State of Decay 3 on the chopping block has been widely criticized, with many seeing it as a prime example of Microsoft's impatience and lack of vision. The game's massive following on Steam, which has eclipsed that of Halo and Gears of War, is a testament to the franchise's enduring popularity and the potential for long-term success that Microsoft is now in danger of missing out on.

The State of Decay series has always been a bit of an outlier in Xbox's portfolio, with its unique blend of survival horror and open-world gameplay resonating with a dedicated but niche audience. However, with the right support and investment, there is no reason why the series couldn't have been nurtured into a major player in the Xbox ecosystem. Instead, Microsoft's decision to abandon the franchise has handed a potential goldmine to a rival company, which will now reap the rewards of the game's popularity. This is a classic case of Microsoft's failure to recognize the value of its own intellectual properties and its tendency to prioritize short-term gains over long-term growth.

The implications of this decision extend far beyond the world of gaming, speaking to a broader issue of Microsoft's corporate culture and its approach to innovation. The company's willingness to cut loose promising projects and franchises in favor of more established properties is a recipe for stagnation and complacency. By prioritizing the tried and true over the new and experimental, Microsoft risks missing out on the next big thing and ceding ground to its competitors. The fact that State of Decay 3 has found a new home and is now poised for success is a stark reminder that sometimes, the best opportunities lie just beyond the boundaries of conventional thinking.

As the dust settles on Xbox's "big reset," it remains to be seen how the company will recover from this self-inflicted wound. The loss of State of Decay 3 is a significant blow, not just in terms of the game's potential earnings, but also in terms of the message it sends to developers and gamers alike. Microsoft's willingness to abandon a beloved franchise and hand it over to a rival company is a stark reminder of the company's sometimes cavalier attitude towards its intellectual properties. As the gaming landscape continues to evolve, Microsoft would do well to take a more nuanced and long-term approach to its portfolio, recognizing the value of its own franchises and investing in the innovation and experimentation that will drive future success.

Looking to the future, it will be interesting to see how State of Decay 3 performs under its new ownership and how Microsoft responds to the loss of this valuable property. One thing is certain, however: the gaming industry is full of unexpected twists and turns, and sometimes, the biggest opportunities arise from the most unlikely places. As the gaming community continues to watch this saga unfold, one thing is clear: the real winners in this story are the fans of State of Decay 3, who will finally get to experience the next installment of this beloved franchise, even if it's not under the Xbox banner.
